message:
feat(demo): add consumes_text_input trait, expand screen keybindings and interactivity

Add Screen::consumes_text_input() trait method (default false) so the app
can suppress single-character global shortcuts (q, ?, m, 0-9) when a
screen is actively receiving text input. Implemented by:
- AdvancedTextEditor: always true (editor always consumes input)
- FormsInput: true when SearchInput, PasswordInput, or TextEditor focused

App-level changes:
- Esc now dismisses the help overlay when visible (before checking a11y panel)
- Chrome: add Ctrl+I evidence inspector to help overlay hints

Screen feature expansion:
- DataViz: add Space (pause/resume animation), r (reset chart data), [PAUSED]
  status bar indicator, and corresponding keybindings/help entries
- InlineModeStory: add d (reset to defaults) with full state reset, reorder
  help entries alphabetically, improve descriptions (e.g. "Toggle chrome
  anchor (top/bottom)", "Adjust log rate (scrollback preserved when mouse
  off)"), add 5 new unit tests covering reset, pause, shortcuts, keybindings,
  and tick-while-paused behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1125,6 +1125,7 @@ fn build_help_overlay_hints(current: ScreenId, screen_bindings: &[HelpEntry]) ->
         .global_entry_categorized("Esc", "Dismiss top overlay", HelpCategory::View)
         .global_entry_categorized("m / F6", "Toggle mouse capture", HelpCategory::View)
         .global_entry_categorized("Ctrl+P", "Toggle performance HUD", HelpCategory::View)
+        .global_entry_categorized("Ctrl+I", "Toggle evidence inspector", HelpCategory::View)
         .global_entry_categorized("Shift+A", "Toggle A11y panel", HelpCategory::View)
         .global_entry_categorized("F12", "Toggle debug overlay", HelpCategory::View)
         // Editing
